title: OLAP Warehouse: ClickHouse / DuckDB (Self-Hosted)
description: High-performance self-hosted columnar OLAP engine. ClickHouse is a distributed columnar database optimised for real-time analytical queries at high concurrency — it can serve thousands of simultaneous analytical queries with sub-100ms p95 on warm data, making it the choice when managed warehouses cannot meet latency or concurrency requirements. DuckDB is an in-process columnar engine for single-node embedded analytics and local data transformation, particularly popular for data engineering scripts and notebook-based analytics. Both are fully open-source (Apache 2.0). ClickHouse Cloud and ClickHouse Altinity provide managed options; DuckDB MotherDuck provides a serverless cloud option.

cost.costNotes: DuckDB: free (open-source), runs on any hardware. ClickHouse self-hosted: cost of compute/storage only. ClickHouse Cloud: $0.36/hr for 30 GiB RAM development tier; production clusters $500-$8,000/mo depending on compute.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min ($50): DuckDB on a $50/mo VM or ClickHouse single-node on a small cloud instance. Max ($10,000): ClickHouse distributed cluster with 6+ nodes (c5.4xlarge ~$600/mo each = $3,600 compute) + EBS storage (~$500/mo) + data transfer + ops tooling. ClickHouse Cloud production cluster with 96 GiB RAM ~$3,000-$8,000/mo depending on concurrency.
provenance.adoptionCost: ClickHouse requires more operational expertise than managed warehouses: cluster provisioning, schema design (choosing MergeTree engine variants, partition keys, ORDER BY), replication topology, and monitoring setup. Estimate 25-30 developer days at ~$160/day = $5,000. DuckDB is significantly simpler for single-node use.
provenance.licenseCost: ClickHouse and DuckDB are Apache 2.0. ClickHouse Cloud and MotherDuck are commercial managed services with no per-seat licensing.

provides.reasoning: ClickHouse delivers sub-second analytical queries at high concurrency through vectorised execution and aggressive data compression. DuckDB provides embedded columnar analytics.
ClickHouse integrates with Grafana, Metabase, Superset, and any tool with a ClickHouse driver. DuckDB integrates directly with Python/R analytics stacks and Jupyter notebooks.
ClickHouse supports high-throughput INSERT batches and Kafka table engine for streaming ingestion. DuckDB can query and transform Parquet, CSV, and JSON files directly.

requires.reasoning: ClickHouse stores data on local disk or S3-compatible object storage (ClickHouse hybrid storage). DuckDB uses local files or DuckDB database files. Both require durable storage.
Self-hosted ClickHouse requires monitoring for merge queue depth, replication lag, and query latency. Prometheus + Grafana is the standard stack.

availability.reason: Open-source pattern deployable on any cloud or on-premises infrastructure

supports_nfr.reason: ClickHouse achieves sub-100ms p95 for analytical queries on warm data with proper schema design (ORDER BY, materialised views). DuckDB achieves sub-10ms for in-process queries. 50ms is the practical floor for network-accessible ClickHouse with non-trivial aggregations.
ClickHouse with ReplicatedMergeTree and ZooKeeper/Keeper achieves 99.9-99.99% availability. Active-active multi-shard with replication can approach five-nines but requires careful topology design.
ClickHouse replica failover with proper replication is 2-5 minutes. Single-node ClickHouse or DuckDB restart from crash is under 1 minute.
ClickHouse replication provides near-zero RPO when replicas are in sync. Backup-based recovery RPO is bounded by backup frequency.

ClickHouse distributed clusters handle up to ~10,000 concurrent analytical queries before network and coordination overhead dominates. DuckDB is single-process with no concurrency limit from the engine itself.
ClickHouse ingestion via batched INSERTs supports up to ~500 batch write operations per second. Real-time streaming via Kafka table engine handles millions of rows/second as large batches internally.
ClickHouse supports username/password, certificate-based mTLS, and LDAP/SAML integration. DuckDB has minimal auth (file-level access control only).
ClickHouse system tables (query_log, query_thread_log) provide detailed audit trails. Compatible with enterprise audit requirements when log export is configured.
ClickHouse supports column-level encryption, row-level access policies, and data masking. PII workloads are supported with appropriate schema and access control design.
Compatible with GDPR when data residency controls, column masking, and right-to-erasure pipeline procedures are in place.
Compatible with HIPAA when warehouse-level encryption and appropriate BAA coverage are configured.
Compatible with CCPA when consumer data deletion triggers a pipeline reprocess that excludes deleted records.
Compatible with SOX when query audit logs provide an immutable trail of data access and transformation operations.
Durability is handled by the underlying object storage layer (S3, GCS, ADLS). All major managed warehouses provide durable storage by default.

supports_constraints.reason: ClickHouse and DuckDB are cloud-agnostic and deployable on any infrastructure including on-premises.
ClickHouse and DuckDB provide JDBC/ODBC drivers and is agnostic to language.
ClickHouse/DuckDB serves as analytical backend for any platform.
Self-hosted OLAP engines are selected specifically for analytical workloads requiring either high concurrency, low latency, or cost-efficiency beyond managed warehouses.

Self-hosted analytical engines require at least minimal operator capacity for provisioning, upgrades, storage management, and incident response. When ops_team_size is zero, prefer managed OLAP services instead.

engine.description: clickhouse: distributed columnar OLAP for production high-concurrency workloads. duckdb: in-process embedded analytics for single-node workloads, data engineering scripts, and notebook environments.

table_engine.description: MergeTree: base engine for non-replicated single-node. ReplicatedMergeTree: HA with ZooKeeper/Keeper replication (recommended for production). SummingMergeTree: auto-aggregate numeric columns. AggregatingMergeTree: materialise aggregates incrementally. ReplacingMergeTree: deduplication by primary key.

replication.description: Enable ReplicatedMergeTree with ZooKeeper/ClickHouse Keeper for HA. Requires minimum 2 ClickHouse nodes and 3 Keeper nodes. Disable for single-node dev/test.

ingestion_mode.description: batch-insert: high-throughput bulk INSERT batches (recommended). kafka-table-engine: native Kafka consumer for streaming ingestion. clickhouse-client-insert: CLI-based bulk loading. http-insert: HTTP interface for language-agnostic clients.

materialized_views.description: Pre-aggregate data at insert time using ClickHouse Materialized Views. Critical for sub-100ms query latency on complex aggregations. Requires upfront schema design to define aggregation targets.
